claims.claim: Pre-LLM routing classifier routes refund vs order-tracking before the main LLM
claims.found: raw transcript: 'classifier ... right before the main LLM as well because maybe you want to check is this something that ... goes to an LLM that handles refunds versus something that handles order tracking perhaps. And so you might have a classifier there'

claims.claim: Post-LLM safety guardrail sits between LLM output and TTS input; you can't take back spoken words
claims.found: raw transcript: 'God reals at the end of the LLM generation before you produce a response' (God reals = mis-transcribed guardrails); 'You can't take back things that are spoken'; 'catching all of those before the TTS model gets invoked is certainly important'

claims.claim: Thinker-talker pattern: small talker LLM emits filler ('let me think about it') and issues a tool call to a bigger thinker model
claims.found: raw transcript: 'thinker talker pattern where you might have a small LLM that is handling all of the conversation ... the response might look like let me think about it'

claims.claim: Together's recommendation: each added model pressures latency -> clear SLAs + independent scaling
claims.found: raw transcript: 'Have very clear sort of guidelines and SLAs on ... how much budget can you really kind of associated with them and then sort of independently scaling them as needed'

claims.claim: Thinker-talker filler masks big-model latency; dead air results if filler runs out before thinker responds (note's elaboration)
claims.found: Transcript supports filler-while-bigger-model-runs (small talker LLM emits 'let me think about it' while a larger model is invoked). 'Dead air if filler runs out' is the note's reasonable inference consistent with the masking mechanism described; not contradicted.

claims.claim: LiveKit's agent_activity.py has no hooks for auxiliary classifiers or guardrails; STT->LLM->TTS is the only path
claims.found: grep for guardrail|classifier|moderation|router|route in agent_activity.py (3895 lines) returns zero matches. The only 'classifier' in the voice dir is an AMD (answering-machine-detection) classifier in voice/amd/detector.py, which is not a routing or content-safety hook.

claims.claim: Pipecat's FrameProcessor chain could insert a classifier processor
claims.found: class FrameProcessor exists in processors/frame_processor.py; processors are chainable via push_frame/process_frame, so a classifier processor is insertable.

claims.claim: No OSS example of a pre-LLM routing classifier / post-LLM guardrail processor exists in any clone
claims.found: Proving a negative. No refund/order LLM-routing or content-safety guardrail example found across livekit voice dir or pipecat src; existing 'classifier' references are turn-detection, IVR, and voicemail detection, not the Together routing/guardrail pattern. Consistent with the note but not strictly provable.

summary: All source claims attributed to together-talk are SUPPORTED verbatim against the raw youtube transcript ground truth (refund-vs-order-tracking pre-LLM classifier, guardrails-after-LLM-before-TTS, can't-take-back-spoken-words, thinker-talker filler, clear SLAs + independent scaling). Clone claims (LiveKit agent_activity.py has no guardrail/classifier hooks; Pipecat FrameProcessor is chainable) are SUPPORTED. The lone soft point is the unprovable 'no OSS example exists' negative -> UNVERIFIABLE. No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
